c.d: The ability of the app to sync and be used seamlessly across multiple devices and operating systems, enhancing accessibility and convenience.
Types of content supported in notes such as text, handwriting, sketches, images, tables, and markdown formatting.
Capability to convert handwritten notes to editable text and integration with stylus devices for natural note-taking.
Options to share notes, collaborate in real time, and manage team workflows within the app.
Ability to access and edit notes without internet connection, ensuring productivity anytime.
Effectiveness of the search function and organizational tools such as tagging, folders, and notebooks that help retrieve and structure notes.
Cost to the user, whether free, freemium, subscription-based, or one-time payment, affecting accessibility and features available.
Simplicity and intuitiveness of the app interface, including distraction-free modes and ease of note-taking.
